perturbation.original_text: Each Party will conduct due diligence with respect to each Permitted Third Party used by such Party to ensure that such Permitted Third Party can comply with all applicable terms and obligations of this Agreement and Applicable Laws.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party will consider conducting due diligence with respect to each Permitted Third Party used by such Party regarding compliance with applicable terms and obligations of this Agreement and Applicable Laws.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will conduct due diligence...to ensure' to 'will consider conducting due diligence regarding compliance' weakens the obligation, making it discretionary rather than mandatory and removing the explicit requirement to 'ensure' compliance. 'Considering' due diligence doesn't ensure actual due diligence, or any specific actions will be taken, creating room for interpretation and potential non-compliance.
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR § 312.52 outlines the requirements for sponsors to select qualified investigators and monitors in clinical trials. By making due diligence discretionary, the modified text conflicts with this regulation's implicit requirement for thorough evaluation of third parties to ensure compliance with trial protocols and patient safety regulations. The word 'consider' does not guarantee adequate oversight, risking violations of GCP.

perturbation.justification: The change from 'will conduct due diligence...to ensure' to 'will consider conducting due diligence regarding compliance' introduces ambiguity, weakening the obligation. The law explanation states that 21 CFR § 312.52 outlines requirements for sponsors to select qualified investigators and monitors in clinical trials. Although Scraped Law Snippet 1 is incomplete, the Law Explanation provided indicates that the change conflicts with the regulation's implicit requirement for thorough evaluation of third parties to ensure compliance. The original text imposes a mandatory obligation to ensure compliance, while the modified text only requires 'consideration', creating a substantial legal contradiction as it undermines a legally expected standard.

perturbation.original_text: PB will use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to oversee the Manufacture of the Product, and PB will materially comply, and PB will require that all Permitted Third Parties of PB materially comply, with all Applicable Laws with respect to the analysis, storage, handling, disposal and transfer of the Product.
perturbation.changed_text: PB will attempt to oversee the Manufacture of the Product, and PB will endeavor to comply, and PB will request that all Permitted Third Parties of PB attempt to comply, with Applicable Laws with respect to the analysis, storage, handling, disposal and transfer of the Product.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts to oversee' with 'attempt to oversee,' 'materially comply' with 'endeavor to comply,' and 'will require ... materially comply' with 'will request ... attempt to comply' significantly weakens the obligations. 'Attempt' and 'endeavor' are subjective and don't guarantee compliance, and a mere 'request' is much weaker than a requirement for material compliance. This introduces ambiguity and allows for varying levels of adherence.
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR § 211.22 mandates written procedures for production and process controls designed to assure that drug products have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess. The modified text weakens the obligation to comply materially with applicable laws, thus conflicting with the strict GMP requirements necessary to ensure product quality and patient safety. 'Attempting' to comply and only 'requesting' compliance from third parties fails to guarantee adherence to these mandatory procedures.

perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity regarding the fulfillment of legal obligations by replacing 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts to oversee' with 'attempt to oversee,' 'materially comply' with 'endeavor to comply,' and 'will require ... materially comply' with 'will request ... attempt to comply.' The Law Explanation states that 21 CFR § 211.22 mandates written procedures for production and process controls designed to assure that drug products have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess. The Scraped Law Snippet 1 confirms that 21 CFR 211.22 pertains to the responsibilities of the quality control unit. The change from a material compliance obligation to an attempted compliance introduces ambiguity, as it doesn't guarantee strict adherence to procedures necessary for product quality and patient safety, directly contradicting the intention of 21 CFR § 211.22.

perturbation.original_text: The Clinical Trials Agreements will also require that the Clinical Investigators, any sub-investigators (e.g., research fellows, residents and associates) and any others required by Applicable Law at each Site complete a financial disclosure document substantially in the form to be agreed upon by the Parties (the "Financial Disclosure Form"). For clarity, if any of the foregoing individuals do not complete such Financial Disclosure Form, such individuals may not participate in, or do any work in connection with, the Clinical Trials.
perturbation.changed_text: The Clinical Trials Agreements may request that the Clinical Investigators, any sub-investigators (e.g., research fellows, residents and associates) and others deemed appropriate at each Site complete a financial disclosure document in a form to be agreed upon by the Parties (the "Financial Disclosure Form"). It is encouraged that the aforementioned individuals complete the Financial Disclosure Form.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will also require' to 'may request' makes the financial disclosure discretionary, not mandatory. Replacing 'any others required by Applicable Law' with 'others deemed appropriate' introduces subjectivity. Removing the clear prohibition of participation without disclosure weakens the control over potential conflicts of interest and compliance with regulatory requirements.
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR § 54.4 mandates that applicants (sponsors) submit certain information concerning the financial interests of clinical investigators. By making the completion of financial disclosure forms discretionary, the modified text contradicts the regulation's requirement for comprehensive disclosure to ensure the integrity and objectivity of clinical trial data. Encouragement doesn't equate to mandatory compliance, increasing the risk of undisclosed conflicts of interest.

perturbation.justification: The original text mandates financial disclosures from clinical investigators and related personnel, while the changed text makes these disclosures discretionary. 21 CFR § 54.4 requires the submission of information concerning the financial interests of clinical investigators. The change from 'will also require' to 'may request' directly weakens this requirement, creating a contradiction. The scraped law snippet indicates that Title 21 deals with regulations, implying the importance of mandatory compliance, which is undermined by the modified text.
